health.upright: For wellbeing, The Tide is a reflective prompt around timing, stress, rest, and sustainable care. Rhythm matters. Notice cycles, energy levels, openings, and natural timing.
health.reversed: For wellbeing, reversed The Tide may point to imbalance around timing. Treat this as a journaling cue, not medical advice. Forcing the tide may waste effort. Missing the tide may come from fear.

name: The Starfield
upright_meaning: Options may be wider than they appear. Hope can be plural.
reversed_meaning: Too many possibilities may blur commitment.
reflection: Which light feels alive, not just impressive?
symbolism: There is more than one light.
yes_no_advice: There is more than one light.

general.upright: Options may be wider than they appear. Hope can be plural.
general.reversed: Too many possibilities may blur commitment.

love.upright: In love and relationships, The Starfield asks how possibility is showing up between people. Options may be wider than they appear. Hope can be plural.
love.reversed: In love and relationships, reversed The Starfield suggests possibility may be blocked, projected, or needing a gentler conversation. Too many possibilities may blur commitment.

work.upright: In work or vocation, The Starfield frames the situation through possibility. Options may be wider than they appear. Hope can be plural.
work.reversed: In work or vocation, reversed The Starfield suggests the pattern around possibility needs clearer expectations, better timing, or a simpler next step. Too many possibilities may blur commitment.

spiritual.upright: Spiritually, The Starfield invites you to treat possibility as a teaching symbol. Options may be wider than they appear. Hope can be plural.
spiritual.reversed: Spiritually, reversed The Starfield asks where your relationship with possibility has become clouded by fear, habit, or over-interpretation. Too many possibilities may blur commitment.

health.reversed: For wellbeing, reversed The Starfield may point to imbalance around possibility. Treat this as a journaling cue, not medical advice. Too many possibilities may blur commitment.
